zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas Cross於1548年出生在英國素福爾可丹納姆時，他的父親James Cross24歲，母親Lady Margery Rotsey30歲。他於1570年與Agnes Napper在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾馬爾費爾德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1647年10月20日在英國格盧斯特斯爾馬爾費爾德享年99歲。

nameMeanings.name: Cross
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived near a cross, such as one set up by the roadside or in a marketplace, from Middle English cros (Old English cros and Old Norse kross, ultimately from Latin crux, crucem). It is commonly Latinized in medieval records as ad crucem and de Cruce but examples of this can just as well belong to the synonymous but less common name Crouch . In a few cases the surname may have been given originally to someone who lived by a crossroads, but this sense of the word seems to have been a comparatively late development. In other cases, the surname (and its European cognates; see 3 below) may have denoted someone who carried the cross in processions of the Christian Church, but in English at least the usual word for this sense was Crozier .
Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘cross’ or ‘the cross’, such as French Lacroix , German Kreutz , and Slovenian and Croatian Križ (see Kriz ).

variants.name: Crouch
meanings: English (southeastern): from Middle English crouch(e), cruch(e) ‘cross’ (Old English crūc, ultimately from Latin crux, crucem; the Old English crūc was replaced in Middle English by the word cross, from Old Norse kross), applied as a topographic name for someone who lived by a cross.

variants.name: Croce
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Angelo, Carlo, Nicola, Salvatore, Vito, Antonio, Gino, Rocco, Saverio, Amadeo, Amerigo.

from croce ‘cross’ (from Latin crux, genitive crucis), applied as a topographic name for someone who lived by a roadside cross, or a nickname for someone who carried the cross in religious processions. Compare English Cross .

variants.name: Grose
meanings: Cornish: topographic name for someone who lived near a stone cross set up by the roadside or in a marketplace, Cornish crous (from Latin crux, crucis). Compare Cross .
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Gross  .

variants.name: Crosse
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Cross  .

variants.name: Kreutz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Bernhard, Gottfried, Orlo, Reinhold.
German: topographic name for someone who lived near a cross set up by the roadside, in a marketplace, or as a field or boundary marker, from Middle High German kriuz(e) ‘cross’. Compare Cross .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas Cross was born in 1548, in Denham, Suffolk, England, his father, James Cross, was 24 and his mother, Lady Margery Rotsey, was 30. He married Agnes Napper in 1570, in Marshfield, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 20 October 1647, in Marshfield, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 99.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Cross(이)가 1548년 영국 수폴크 데함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Cross(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Margery Rotsey(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1570년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 마스h필드에서 Agnes Napper(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1647년 10월 20일 영국 그루스스터실르 마스h필드에서 99세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas Cross родился в 1548 г. в местности денам, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени James Cross было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Margery Rotsey, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Napper в 1570 г. в местности маршфиелд, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 20 октября 1647 г. в местности маршфиелд, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 99 лет.
